summary: Full responsive audit of IMEX AI at widths 320/375/390/414/768/1024/1440 across /dashboard, /calculator, /import-vs-local, /profit-mode, /opportunities, /assistant. All 36 (width x route) checks pass no-horizontal-scroll (scrollWidth == clientWidth). Mobile hamburger nav (< 1024px) shows correctly, desktop sidebar (>= 1024px) shows correctly and the two never overlap. Drawer opens via mobile-nav-toggle, closes on link click (auto-close on route change via useEffect on location.pathname), on mobile-nav-close, and on mobile-nav-backdrop click. All 6 per-link testids (nav-*-mobile) present and navigate correctly. Calculator at 375px is fully single-column (all 8 inputs share the same x-coordinate); calc-submit still renders calc-result on mobile. Trade Volume chart on /dashboard fits within 375px viewport (width 285px). Field inputs/selects render at 49px min-height (>= 44). No console errors on any tested route. Import vs Local (5 inputs) and Profit Mode (6 inputs) are also single-column on mobile with 49px min height. Desktop 1440 correctly hides mobile-nav-toggle and shows all nav-* sidebar links; clicking nav-calculator navigates to /calculator.

issues: Brand button data-testid=nav-landing has a computed height of 40px on mobile — slightly under the 44px tap-target guideline. It is a nav/logo button (not a form input), so this does not violate the explicit requirement (which targets Field inputs/selects), but bumping it to h-11 would improve tap ergonomics.

action_items: (Optional, non-blocking) Increase mobile brand button (nav-landing) height from h-10 (40px) to h-11 (44px) in MobileNav.jsx line ~36 to fully satisfy the 44px tap-target guideline. Nothing else required — responsive spec is fully met.
critical_code_review_comments: MobileNav.jsx: cleanly uses AnimatePresence + framer-motion tween for the drawer; useEffect on location.pathname to auto-close on navigation is the right pattern. body scroll lock on open is a nice touch.
Sidebar and MobileNav share navLinks.js — good DRY. Both use NavLink so isActive styling is consistent.
App.js: overflow-x-hidden on the shell + min-w-0 on <main> is the correct combination to prevent flex children from bursting the viewport.
Field.jsx: min-h-[48px] applied to both input and select — good and matches the 44px+ tap target requirement.
index.css body overflow-x:hidden is a safe belt-and-suspenders; combined with the App shell class it fully eliminates horizontal scroll across 320-1440px.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (36/36 no-overflow, drawer 3/3 close paths, 6/6 mobile nav links, 6/6 desktop nav links, calculator single-col + submit + result render, 44px tap targets on all inputs)

seed_data_creation: None. Used prefilled defaults on the Landed Cost form.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Responsive rework fully verified. MobileNav renders below 1024px, Sidebar renders at/above 1024px. All 6 workspace routes have zero horizontal overflow at 320/375/390/414/768/1024/1440. Drawer testids (mobile-nav-toggle/drawer/close/backdrop and per-link *-mobile) all wired. Calculator/Import vs Local/Profit Mode forms all single-column on 375px. Only cosmetic nit: brand button in MobileNav header is 40px vs ideal 44px; not required by spec.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found in this iteration. The previously implemented AppShell (overflow-x-hidden + min-w-0 main), MobileNav (hidden lg:flex hamburger + framer-motion drawer), Sidebar (hidden lg:flex), Field (min-h-[48px]) collectively deliver all responsive requirements.
